page: Microsoft Azure M128m is an MS family memory-intensive server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 3892.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 30.41 GB per core. It includes 14680 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests. Conversely, multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with large block read-only bandwidth reaching 382.78 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% tier. Large block write and small block cached read bandwidths also rank in the top 25%.

Given its massive memory capacity and high local storage, this server class is designed for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw single-threaded compute. It demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, text generation for large language models is poor, though prompt processing remains average. Qualitatively, the hardware profile offers high resource density for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications that require massive RAM footprints and substantial local storage, offsetting the lower single-core efficiency.
description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-intensive server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8280M cores, running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 3892.0 GB of RAM, yielding 30.41 GB per core, and includes 14680 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server exhibits a distinct performance tradeoff: single-core CPU benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are highly competitive. Specifically, large block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 382.78 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% tier. Multi-core stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores rank in the top 25%. This profile makes the server highly effective for memory-heavy workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and static web serving, where massive memory capacity and high multi-threaded throughput are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-intensive server featuring 128 vCPUs, 3892 GB of RAM, and 14680 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth alongside strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-intensive server with 128 vCPUs, 3892 GB RAM, and 14680 GB local storage, optimized for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive platform combining massive RAM capacity and high local storage with strong multi-core throughput for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 3892.0 GB of RAM providing 30.41 GB per core
14680 GB of local storage for large-scale data retention
Top-tier large block read-only memory bandwidth at 382.78 GB/sec
Strong multi-core CPU performance and Redis SET operations
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and static web serving
